description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two jobs: build and verify_signatures. Use the custom Docker image registry.gitlab.com/robigalia/runner:latest for the build job, whose before_script runs git submodule update --init --recursive and clones the sel4-config, sel4, sel4-start, rust-sel4, and sel4-sys repositories, then its script executes pushd ../sel4; make x86_defconfig; popd; ./build.sh --target i686-sel4-robigalia; pushd ../sel4; make x64_qemu_defconfig; popd; ./build.sh --target x86_64-sel4-robigalia. Use the ubuntu:17.10 image for the verify_signatures job, which runs apt update; apt install --assume-yes python-pexpect gnupg2 git; git clone --depth=1 https://gitlab.com/robigalia/meta; gpg2 --list-keys; (gpg2 --export --no-default-keyring --keyring ./meta/.gnupg/pubring.kbx || true) | gpg2 --import; gpg2 --list-keys --fingerprint --fingerprint --verbose; ./meta/verify-signatures.py HEAD ./meta/.mailmap. Both jobs run in the default stage without additional caches, artifacts, or rules.
